title: Running a simulation
text: vignette show create single simulation using basic functions package. create DCM simulation need: test, Q-matrix examinees. can quickly using saltr.

title: Running a simulation
text: Let’s first create Q-matrix. create_qmatrix objective give us fast template Q-matrix can modify. Let’s make item 9 measure attributes. Perfect. far, good. Now, want create test compatible Q-matrix. normally tricky thing , using build_test_parameters operation simplified. pass extra arguments, result build_test_parameters just mask parameters present test. columns represent attribute parameters. can also pass values want interaction level starting intercept. way , function greatly simplifies creation starting template test can adjusted. Notice 3-way interaction effect item 9 0 NA, important compatibility matrix. example, let’s make one main effects items 6 7 smaller.
code: library(saltr)  qmatrix <- create_qmatrix(num_attr=3, items_per_type = c(2,1)) qmatrix #>       Attr1 Attr2 Attr3 #> Item1     1     0     0 #> Item2     1     0     0 #> Item3     0     1     0 #> Item4     0     1     0 #> Item5     0     0     1 #> Item6     0     0     1 #> Item7     1     1     0 #> Item8     1     0     1 #> Item9     0     1     1 qmatrix[9,] <- c(1,1,1) # We pass our qmatrix as an argument  test <- build_test_parameters(qmatrix) test #>       0 1 2 3 1-2 1-3 2-3 1-2-3 #> Item1 1 1 0 0   0   0   0     0 #> Item2 1 1 0 0   0   0   0     0 #> Item3 1 0 1 0   0   0   0     0 #> Item4 1 0 1 0   0   0   0     0 #> Item5 1 0 0 1   0   0   0     0 #> Item6 1 0 0 1   0   0   0     0 #> Item7 1 1 1 0   1   0   0     0 #> Item8 1 1 0 1   0   1   0     0 #> Item9 1 1 1 1   1   1   1     1  # Item 8 has the intercept 0, the main effects 1 and 3,  # and the two-way interaction 1-3 test[8,] #>     0     1     2     3   1-2   1-3   2-3 1-2-3  #>     1     1     0     1     0     1     0     0 # Only intercept and main effects test <- build_test_parameters(qmatrix, -2, 2) test #>        0  1  2  3 1-2 1-3 2-3 1-2-3 #> Item1 -2  2 NA NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item2 -2  2 NA NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item3 -2 NA  2 NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item4 -2 NA  2 NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item5 -2 NA NA  2  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item6 -2 NA NA  2  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item7 -2  2  2 NA   0  NA  NA    NA #> Item8 -2  2 NA  2  NA   0  NA    NA #> Item9 -2  2  2  2   0   0   0     0  # Intercept, main effects and two-way interactions test <- build_test_parameters(qmatrix, -2, 2, .5) test #>        0  1  2  3 1-2 1-3 2-3 1-2-3 #> Item1 -2  2 NA NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item2 -2  2 NA NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item3 -2 NA  2 NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item4 -2 NA  2 NA  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item5 -2 NA NA  2  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item6 -2 NA NA  2  NA  NA  NA    NA #> Item7 -2  2  2 NA 0.5  NA  NA    NA #> Item8 -2  2 NA  2  NA 0.5  NA    NA #> Item9 -2  2  2  2 0.5 0.5 0.5     0 test[6, 4] <- 1 test[7, 3] <- 1  # Using pretty_print makes it easier to read pretty_print(test) #>         λ0      λ1      λ2      λ3     λ1-2    λ1-3    λ2-3   λ1-2-3  #> Item1    -2.0     2.0                                                 #> Item2    -2.0     2.0                                                 #> Item3    -2.0             2.0                                         #> Item4    -2.0             2.0                                         #> Item5    -2.0                     2.0                                 #> Item6    -2.0                     1.0                                 #> Item7    -2.0     2.0     1.0             0.5                         #> Item8    -2.0     2.0             2.0             0.5                 #> Item9    -2.0     2.0     2.0     2.0     0.5     0.5     0.5     0.0

title: Running a simulation
text: Great! now test need examinees. function random component use generate_* function. function wrapper bindata::rmvbin extra logic errors conciseness. example, one value passed, assumes . important attribute strict, FALSE, attribute correlation assumed correlation underlying normal variables. default value TRUE, meaning try create binary matrix follows restrictions.possible throw error.
code: examinees <- generate_examinees(   1000, # sample size   total_attrs = 3,   base_rate = .5, # All three attributes will have the same base-rate   attr_corr = c(.3, 0, 0) )  # Checking results colMeans(examinees) #> Attr1 Attr2 Attr3  #> 0.488 0.499 0.502 cor(examinees) |> round(2) #>       Attr1 Attr2 Attr3 #> Attr1  1.00  0.34  0.02 #> Attr2  0.34  1.00  0.00 #> Attr3  0.02  0.00  1.00 # This will throw an error sample_1 <- generate_examinees(   1000,    total_attrs = 3,   base_rate = c(.8, .5, .2),   attr_corr = c(.8, .1, 0),   strict = TRUE ) #> Error in `generate_examinees()`: #> ! Invalid joint probability of vars 1 and 2. Allowed range: [0.30, 0.50], but got: 0.56  # This wont sample_2 <- generate_examinees(   1000,    total_attrs = 3,   base_rate = c(.8, .5, .2),   attr_corr = c(.8, .1, 0),   strict = FALSE )  # Although it is always better to pass some value for tolerance # To get info on the generation. sample_3 <- generate_examinees(   1000,    total_attrs = 3,   base_rate = c(.8, .5, .2),   attr_corr = c(.8, .1, 0),   tolerance = .1 ) #> Warning in generate_examinees(1000, total_attrs = 3, base_rate = c(0.8, : 1 correlation values are outside tolerance range #> Expected: 0.8, 0.1, 0 #> Obtained: 0.439, 0.081, 0.007

title: Running a simulation
text: Now test examinees can generate responses. need pass Q-matrix information already encoded test. way ’s less chances making mistakes. nice feature function can help us avoid mistakes happened modification test. example, inadvertently added second main effect item 2. now can just fit model.
code: responses <- generate_responses(examinees, test) wrong_test <- test  # Now item 2 has a main effect but the interaction 1-2 is NA  wrong_test[2,3] <- .5  responses <- generate_responses(examinees, wrong_test) #> Error in `generate_responses()`: #> ! The test is not properly formed

title: Running a simulation
text: complete code start zero get simulated responses test just couple lines!
code: qmatrix <- create_qmatrix(num_attr=3, items_per_type = c(2,1)) qmatrix[9,] <- c(1,1,1)   test <- build_test_parameters(qmatrix, -2, 2, .5) test[6, 4] <- 1 test[7, 3] <- 1  examinees <- generate_examinees(   1000,    total_attrs = 3,   base_rate = .5,    attr_corr = c(.3, 0, 0) )  responses <- generate_responses(examinees, test)

title: Jump start your DCM simulation work
text: goal saltr (pronounced salt-er, like word jump Spanish), help create readable simulations use semantically meaningful functions. allow researchers use flexible way without needing commit fully package.

title: Jump start your DCM simulation work
text: lot statistics libraries make heavy use objects. allows packages handle things neatly avoid errors can hard debug user side. downside , however, closed systems stiff black boxes, time require lot custom code steeper learning curve. Saltr library aims give researchers, DCM field, tools need jump start simulations making easier work matrices gdina objects CDM package. Saltr designed following tidyverse style guide functions actions designed work nicely pipe operator |>. Additionally, functions try implement meaningful warning error messages help fix bugs coding process.

title: Jump start your DCM simulation work
text: Saltr built around 5 function families identified suffix: create_*, build_*, generate_*, get_*, change_*. create_* functions mostly utility functions. take simple arguments help create useful objects. example, create_qmatrix makes faster create starting Q-matrix can modified. build_* functions always take input matrix return object related. example, can create test consistent Q-matrix calling build_test_parameters. functions can also useful build theoretical results like build_response_likelihood. generate_* functions group deterministic, hence name. functions bread butter simulation study. use create random samples examinees (generate_examinees) responses (generate_responses). far families functions package agnostic, simulation moment want fit DCM model extract information. information readily available, can cumbersome require extra wrangling. get_* functions simplify extraction information model. Finally, change_* family takes object returns modified version . Useful changes objects relevant study like change_qmatrix change_test_parameters simplify studying, example, misspecification model.

code: # example code # This creates a 4 attribute qmatrix with 3 items per attribute. create_qmatrix(4, 3) #>        Attr1 Attr2 Attr3 Attr4 #> Item1      1     0     0     0 #> Item2      1     0     0     0 #> Item3      1     0     0     0 #> Item4      0     1     0     0 #> Item5      0     1     0     0 #> Item6      0     1     0     0 #> Item7      0     0     1     0 #> Item8      0     0     1     0 #> Item9      0     0     1     0 #> Item10     0     0     0     1 #> Item11     0     0     0     1 #> Item12     0     0     0     1  # This creates a 3 attribute qmatrix with 2 simple items per attribute, # 1 double attribute item per combination, and 1 triple attribute item. create_qmatrix(3, c(2, 1, 1)) #>        Attr1 Attr2 Attr3 #> Item1      1     0     0 #> Item2      1     0     0 #> Item3      0     1     0 #> Item4      0     1     0 #> Item5      0     0     1 #> Item6      0     0     1 #> Item7      1     1     0 #> Item8      1     0     1 #> Item9      0     1     1 #> Item10     1     1     1
